audit_text: Copy-text on linkified spans. linkify.ts renders plain <a href> with no clipboard intercept. Copying a workspace-relative link gives the expanded file:/// href, not the visible text. Spec (tool-output-streaming.md §Clickable file paths): copy copies visible text, not href.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md §"Clickable file paths and URLs in output" — "Selecting and copying a linkified span copies the visible text (the path or URL as the tool printed it), not the underlying href."
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/linkify.ts:271-280 (segmentToHtml emits `${displayText}` for file segments)
frontend/src/lib/sanitize.ts:80-146 (allowlists data-link-kind + file:// href; no copy-event hook installed)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/MessageTurn.svelte:416 ({@html sanitizeHtml(linkifyToHtml(turn.body))})
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/ToolOutput.svelte:223 ({@html sanitizeHtml(linkifyToHtml(call.output, { workingDir }))})
verdict_evidence: linkifyToHtml emits anchors whose innerText is the displayText (the path the tool printed) and whose href is the resolved file:// URL — for absolute paths the two differ by the file:// scheme, for workspace-relative paths the href is fully expanded against workingDir while displayText keeps the relative form. No copy event listener is installed in MessageTurn.svelte, ToolOutput.svelte, sanitize.ts, or any conversation-pane component (grep for `oncopy|onCopy|navigator.clipboard.writeText` inside the linkified surfaces returns no override). Default browser selection-copy puts innerText on the plaintext clipboard, which already matches the spec; however the rich-text/HTML clipboard slot still carries the file:// href, and applications that paste rich-text (most Markdown-aware editors, Slack, Notion) surface the expanded href rather than the visible path. The audit's literal observation reproduces in those paste contexts. No commit since the audit added a copy intercept or a regression test pinning the spec guarantee.
acceptance_criteria: A `copy` event handler installed on the conversation/tool-output rendering surface intercepts selection copies that include linkified spans and writes only the visible-text (path-as-printed) to both the text/plain and text/html clipboard slots.

For a workspace-relative anchor (e.g. `frontend/src/lib/x.svelte` displayed text, `file:///abs/.../frontend/src/lib/x.svelte` href), copying the selection results in clipboard text/plain = the relative path and text/html does NOT contain the file:// href.
A regression test (jsdom + simulated `copy` event with a Selection covering a file segment) asserts the clipboardData.setData('text/plain', ...) call carries the displayText and not the href.
Plain text outside any anchor still copies unchanged.
executor_brief: linkifyToHtml renders file links as `relative/path`. Plain-text selection-copy already gives the visible path, but rich-text paste surfaces the file:// href — the spec says copy should emit visible text only. Add a `copy` event handler on the conversation pane (or on the linkified surfaces in MessageTurn / ToolOutput) that rewrites the clipboard payload so both text/plain and text/html slots carry the displayText, and add a vitest case that simulates the copy event over a file segment.

audit_text: ANSI passthrough. DOMPurify strips ANSI escapes. Spec says they pass through and render as colored text. Bash tool output loses all color. No ANSI-to-color renderer wired anywhere.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md §"What the user does NOT see" — "ANSI color codes the tool emits are rendered as colored text inline". Linkifier note in §"Clickable file paths and URLs in output" — "ANSI escape sequences are passed through unchanged; this feature does not introduce ANSI colorisation" defers ANSI rendering to a separate layer that is not present.
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/sanitize.ts:49-146 (allowlist of tags/attrs; no ANSI parser; FORBID_TAGS includes <style>)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/ToolOutput.svelte:223 (renders sanitized linkified HTML — no ANSI translation upstream of sanitize)
frontend/src/lib/linkify.ts:266-280 (linkifyToHtml — no ANSI handling)
package.json (no ansi-to-html / anser / chalk-styles dependency)
verdict_evidence: No frontend module translates ANSI escape sequences (CSI `\x1b[...m`) into styled HTML. The rendering pipeline for tool output is `linkifyToHtml(call.output, …)` → `sanitizeHtml(...)` → `{@html ...}` inside a `<pre>`. linkifyToHtml only escapes for HTML and emits anchors; sanitize only prunes tags/attrs. ANSI bytes survive both stages and render literally as `\x1b[31m` glyph runs in the `<pre>`. The audit's claim that DOMPurify *strips* ANSI is technically wrong about the mechanism (DOMPurify acts on tags/attrs, not byte patterns) but the user-visible defect — bash-tool colour absent — is real. No ANSI-rendering library is in package.json and no bespoke parser exists in src/lib/.
suggested_files_to_change: frontend/package.json (if pulling a vetted ansi-to-html library)
acceptance_criteria: A new module (or vetted library) converts ANSI CSI SGR escape sequences into safely-styled `<span>` elements covering at least the 8/16-colour foreground/background palette, bold, italic, underline, and reset.
ToolOutput.svelte's render pipeline applies the ANSI translator BEFORE linkifyToHtml/sanitizeHtml so escape bytes are gone by the time DOMPurify runs.

sanitize.ts's allowlist permits the `<span>` tag plus the specific class/style attributes the ANSI translator emits (CSS class names from a fixed enum preferred over inline `style` for sanitizer simplicity).

A vitest case feeds bash-style coloured output (e.g. `\x1b[31mFAIL\x1b[0m ok`) and asserts the rendered HTML contains a styled span around `FAIL` with the red foreground class and a plain text run for ` ok`.
Linkified file paths interleaved with ANSI runs still resolve correctly (combined fixture).

Streaming-time chunking (runner.py STREAM_MAX_DELTA_CHARS) preserves ANSI escape boundaries — no half-escape mid-chunk; multi-byte safety promise in tool-output-streaming.md §Very-long-output truncation rules holds.
executor_brief: Bash tool output reaches the conversation pane with raw ANSI escapes intact, rendered literally as `\x1b[31m…` runs because no ANSI-to-HTML translator is wired. Add a small ANSI SGR parser (or pull a vetted library) that emits class-styled spans, run it on tool output before linkify+sanitize, extend the sanitizer allowlist to admit the ANSI span shape, and add a vitest case that asserts coloured runs render as styled spans. Verify the existing chunk-boundary safety in runner.py still holds (no escape sequence is split mid-chunk).

audit_text: Soft-cap expander missing. runner.py only implements hard-cap truncation marker. Spec (tool-output-streaming.md §Very-long-output truncation rules) requires two tiers: soft cap folds middle into inline Show full output expander; hard cap truncates at end. Soft tier absent.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md §"Very-long-output truncation rules" — "Soft cap on display. Output beyond the soft cap is folded inside an inline 'Show full output' expander; the visible head/tail bookends remain interactive (selectable, copyable). The full body is still streamed and persisted; the expander simply collapses the middle for layout."
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/agent/runner.py:489-528 (_emit_tool_output_delta — only hard cap STREAM_MAX_TOOL_OUTPUT_CHARS + STREAM_TRUNCATION_MARKER_TEMPLATE)
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts:362-383 (hydrateToolCalls — slice(rawLength - CHAT_TOOL_OUTPUT_SOFT_CAP_CHARS) tail-only)
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts:762-779 (live tool_output_delta reducer — same tail-only slice)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/ToolOutput.svelte:1-260 (no Show-full-output expander UI surface)
frontend/src/lib/config.ts:535,652 (toolOutputExpand string + CHAT_TOOL_OUTPUT_SOFT_CAP_CHARS=8000 — string defined but never wired to a button)
verdict_evidence: Frontend has a soft cap of 8000 chars (CHAT_TOOL_OUTPUT_SOFT_CAP_CHARS) that head-truncates: `merged.slice(merged.length - CHAT_TOOL_OUTPUT_SOFT_CAP_CHARS)` (conversation.svelte.ts:771-773 and 365-367). This drops the head entirely and never exposes the elided portion through any expander — `elidedCount` is computed in ToolOutput.svelte:123 (`call.rawLength - call.output.length`) but is only used for an `[X chars elided]` annotation, not a Show-full button. The string `toolOutputExpand: "Show full output"` exists in config.ts:535 but no component imports it. Backend (runner.py) implements only the hard cap marker. Spec demands head AND tail bookends with a middle-folding expander whose click reveals the persisted full body — both the head visibility and the expander UI are absent. The full body is also not persisted past the hard cap, so even if the expander were added, the unfolded content would be unreachable for sessions that exceeded both caps; the soft cap therefore needs its own persistence path (full body retained on the tool_calls row, only the display is folded).
suggested_files_to_change: src/bearings/config/constants.py (introduce STREAM_TOOL_OUTPUT_SOFT_CAP_CHARS named constant separate from the hard cap)
acceptance_criteria: A named soft-cap constant lives in bearings.config.constants (e.g. STREAM_TOOL_OUTPUT_SOFT_CAP_CHARS) distinct from STREAM_MAX_TOOL_OUTPUT_CHARS and is the single source of truth (frontend mirrors it via config.ts; no inline literal).
Soft-cap rendering shows the head bookend (first N/2 chars), the tail bookend (last N/2 chars), and a `Show full output` button inline between them.
Clicking `Show full output` reveals the full persisted middle in place; clicking again (or a `Collapse` affordance) refolds it.
The full pre-hard-cap body remains persisted on the tool_calls row so the expander has content to reveal across page reloads.

A vitest case asserts the head/tail/expander structure renders for output > soft cap and < hard cap; another asserts the hard-cap [truncated — N bytes elided] marker still appears at the tail end of the persisted body for output > hard cap.
A pytest case asserts runner.py's tool-output-delta path preserves the full pre-hard-cap body (no head dropping) so frontend hydration can reconstruct head + middle + tail.
executor_brief: Spec mandates a two-tier truncation: soft cap folds the middle into an inline 'Show full output' expander with visible head/tail bookends; hard cap appends the [truncated — N bytes elided] marker. Backend has only the hard cap; frontend has a head-dropping slice that's not what the spec wants. Add a separate soft-cap constant, persist the full pre-hard-cap body, render the head/tail/expander UI in ToolOutput.svelte, and add tests on both sides. Coordinate with #2 (ANSI) so the expander operates on already-translated styled spans rather than raw bytes.

audit_text: [stopped] annotation missing. When turn stopped, partial assistant bubble should gain [stopped] annotation. Nothing in backend or frontend adds it. Spec: chat.md §Stopping or interrupting a turn.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/chat.md §"Stopping or interrupting a turn" — "Pressing it interrupts the agent at the next safe boundary; the partially-streamed assistant bubble is preserved with a `[stopped]` annotation."
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/agent/sdk_loop.py:191-204 (_stop_watcher — calls session.interrupt(), emits no annotation event)
src/bearings/agent/events.py:1-320 (no StoppedAnnotation / message_stopped event type)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/MessageTurn.svelte (no [stopped] annotation render in assistant bubble)
grep `\[stopped\]|stoppedAnnotation` over src/bearings + frontend/src — only the spec text and audit text match
verdict_evidence: No backend event type signals a stopped turn (events.py defines MessageComplete with no `stopped` flag and no separate Stopped event). _stop_watcher calls session.interrupt() but emits nothing on the runner. Frontend MessageTurn.svelte renders the assistant bubble body but has no conditional [stopped] suffix or chip. Grep across both halves of the codebase confirms zero references to a stopped annotation. Audit reproduced verbatim. STILL_OPEN.
suggested_files_to_change: src/bearings/agent/events.py (add `stopped: bool` to MessageComplete or a new TurnStopped event)
src/bearings/agent/sdk_loop.py (emit the stopped flag/event on interrupt path)
src/bearings/web/models/messages.py (carry the flag through to MessageOut)
src/bearings/db/messages.py (persist a `stopped` column or derive at insert)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/MessageTurn.svelte (render `[stopped]` annotation when the assistant turn was interrupted)
acceptance_criteria: A new MessageComplete field `stopped: bool` (or equivalent TurnStopped sub-event) is set true when the turn ended via the stop_event path, false otherwise.
The flag round-trips through persistence (assistant message row), the messages REST/WS payloads, and the frontend message store.
MessageTurn.svelte renders a `[stopped]` annotation chip/suffix on the assistant bubble when the flag is true; not rendered otherwise.
A pytest case drives a turn through interrupt and asserts the persisted assistant message row has `stopped=true` and the wire envelope carries the flag.
A vitest case renders an assistant turn fixture with `stopped=true` and asserts the [stopped] annotation appears with the configured string from config.ts.
Reload of a stopped session reattaches and the [stopped] annotation persists (not just live-stream visible).
executor_brief: Stopping a turn currently leaves the assistant bubble unannotated — the spec requires a `[stopped]` annotation on the partial bubble. Plumb a `stopped` boolean through the assistant-message lifecycle: set it on the interrupt path in sdk_loop.py, persist it on the messages row, surface it in MessageOut, mirror in the frontend message store, and render the annotation in MessageTurn.svelte. Tests on both sides; the i18n string lives in config.ts.

audit_text: Stop undo semantics inverted. StopUndoInline.svelte implements a pre-stop cancel (Undo prevents the stop POST). Spec says: stop fires, then Undo re-issues the same prompt. Opposite behavior. Distinct defect class — feature inverted.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/chat.md §"Stopping or interrupting a turn" — "Pressing it interrupts the agent at the next safe boundary; the partially-streamed assistant bubble is preserved with a `[stopped]` annotation. The user can immediately type a new message — the session is ready for the next turn. A small 'undo stop' inline appears for a few seconds after stopping, letting the user re-issue the same prompt without retyping it."
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/StopUndoInline.svelte:1-163 (handleStop arms a STOP_UNDO_GRACE_MS setTimeout that fires the /stop POST only on expiry; handleUndo cancels the timer so the POST never fires)
frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts (stopSession definition — single POST /stop call; no companion 'redrive' call surfaced for an Undo-after-stop flow)
frontend/src/lib/config.ts (STOP_UNDO_GRACE_MS / STOP_UNDO_TICK_MS / STOP_UNDO_LABEL strings — all wired to the pre-stop debounce model)
verdict_evidence: StopUndoInline.svelte:84-103 starts a grace-window timer when the user clicks Stop and only POSTs `/stop` when the timer expires; clicking Undo (handleUndo, L100-103) clears the timers and aborts the POST. This is the literal inverse of the spec semantics: spec wants the stop to fire IMMEDIATELY, the assistant bubble to gain `[stopped]`, and a transient Undo to re-issue the original prompt to the prompt endpoint. The component's docstring at L6-14 even names this 'gap-cycle-11-002' as a deliberate behaviour change — but it does not match chat.md §Stopping or interrupting a turn. Verbatim audit reproduced; fixed by either rewriting the component to the spec model (immediate POST + redrive) or amending chat.md to legitimise the grace-window. The audit's classification calls this STILL_OPEN, and HEAD is unchanged.
suggested_files_to_change: frontend/src/lib/api/prompt.ts (or sessions.ts — wire a redrive helper that re-POSTs the last user prompt)
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts (track the last user-bubble content so the redrive has a payload)
docs/behavior/chat.md (only if the team chooses to amend the spec; verifier flags this as the alternative)
acceptance_criteria: Clicking Stop fires `POST /api/sessions/{id}/stop` immediately (no grace window).
After the stop POST resolves, an inline 'Undo stop' affordance appears for a configurable grace window (e.g. ~6 s).
Clicking Undo within the window POSTs the original user prompt's content via the prompt endpoint, restoring the conversation flow without retyping.
Undo grace window expiry simply hides the affordance — no further action.
If a new message is sent during the Undo window, the affordance hides immediately (the user moved on).
vitest cases cover (a) Stop click → POST /stop fires synchronously, (b) Undo click within window → POST /prompt with the captured prior-turn content, (c) window expiry → no further side-effects.
[stopped] annotation (finding feature-2-004) lands the partial bubble correctly across the immediate stop path.
executor_brief: StopUndoInline implements the inverse of the spec: it debounces the Stop POST behind a grace window and Undo cancels the pending POST. Spec wants the Stop to fire immediately and an Undo affordance afterwards that redrives the original prompt. Rewrite handleStop to POST /stop synchronously, surface a post-stop Undo affordance for a few seconds, wire Undo to POST /prompt with the previous user-bubble content (the conversation store already has it), and add vitest coverage. Coordinate with finding feature-2-004 so the immediate-stop path also drops the [stopped] annotation.

audit_text: ToolProgress event dead code. events.py defines it, nothing emits it. Elapsed timer in ToolOutput.svelte advances only from client setInterval. Backgrounded-tab keepalive path non-functional.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md §"Long-tool keepalive" — "Even with no output bytes arriving, a periodic keepalive event keeps the per-tab UI reactive (so a backgrounded tab doesn't render a stale state when refocused mid-call)."
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/agent/events.py:121-128 (ToolProgress class — `tool_call_id`, `elapsed_ms`)
src/bearings/config/constants.py:153-157 (TOOL_PROGRESS_INTERVAL = 2s — comment says 'Per-tool-call ProgressTickerManager emits a heartbeat at this cadence')
grep `ToolProgress\(` over src/bearings — only events.py:121 (definition); no production emitter; only tests/test_session_events.py:92 and tests/test_streaming_unit.py:89 construct it manually
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts:788-792 (consumer reducer for type==='tool_progress' updates liveElapsedMs)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/ToolOutput.svelte:107-115 (local setInterval ticks nowMs every 1s — no server-driven keepalive)
verdict_evidence: ToolProgress is defined and consumed but never emitted. No `ProgressTickerManager` class exists in src/bearings — the constants.py docstring at L153-156 references it as the intended emitter but the module is absent. The frontend reducer for tool_progress events therefore never fires, leaving liveElapsedMs at 0 across the lifetime of any tool call. ToolOutput.svelte's 1s setInterval keeps the readout ticking only while the tab is foregrounded; backgrounded tabs throttle setInterval to ≥1s and may delay it past 60s, causing the readout to stale exactly the way the keepalive was meant to prevent. Audit reproduced; STILL_OPEN.
suggested_files_to_change: src/bearings/agent/sdk_loop.py (start/stop the ticker around the tool-call window)
tests/test_runner.py (or wherever runner emit paths are covered)
acceptance_criteria: A per-session (or per-tool-call) ticker emits ToolProgress events at TOOL_PROGRESS_INTERVAL_S cadence for every in-flight tool call.
The ticker starts on ToolCallStart and stops on ToolCallEnd; multiple concurrent tool calls each get their own elapsed_ms tick.

Emitted ToolProgress events are NOT ring-buffered (the spec line: 'The keepalive is not part of the persisted record. A reconnecting client picks up the live timer from the next live tick rather than replaying keepalives.') — a separate fan-out path is used so ring-buffer replay does not include them.

A pytest case starts a ToolCallStart, advances simulated time by N ticks, and asserts the queue receives N ToolProgress events with monotonically-increasing elapsed_ms and the correct tool_call_id, without ring-buffer replay duplication.
A pytest case asserts ToolProgress events are absent from `runner.subscribe(since_seq=0)` replay output.
executor_brief: ToolProgress is wired into the schema and the frontend reducer but no production emitter exists — the constants comment names a `ProgressTickerManager` that was never built. Add a ticker that emits ToolProgress per in-flight tool call at TOOL_PROGRESS_INTERVAL_S, started by ToolCallStart and stopped by ToolCallEnd, fan-out only (not ring-buffered per the spec's 'keepalive is not part of the persisted record' rule). Tests on both lifecycle and replay-exclusion paths. Coordinate with finding feature-2-007 — the ticker is the natural surface for the server-authoritative elapsed floor on reconnect.

audit_text: Elapsed timer inflates on long-disconnect replay. Server-reported timestamp path for in-flight tools absent. Keepalive heartbeats carry no tool_call_id and are not ring-buffered.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md §"Reconnect / replay during a stream" — "The elapsed-time readout uses the client clock during the live phase and a server-reported timestamp on first replay so a long disconnect doesn't show a wildly inflated client-side timer."
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/agent/events.py:86-98 (ToolCallStart — no started_at server timestamp field)
src/bearings/web/serialize.py:76,107-110 (heartbeat_frame: `{kind:'heartbeat', ts:<float>}` — no tool_call_id)
src/bearings/web/streaming.py:146-148 (heartbeat dispatched on idle, NOT through runner.emit, NOT ring-buffered)
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts:740-761 (tool_call_start reducer — idempotent on tool_call_id; preserves the original startedAt across replay)
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts:757 (`startedAt: Date.now()` set at first receive of tool_call_start)
frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/ToolOutput.svelte:117-122 (`elapsedMs = Math.max(nowMs - call.startedAt, call.liveElapsedMs)` — both terms are client-clock-derived)
verdict_evidence: ToolCallStart carries no server timestamp; the frontend reducer stamps `startedAt = Date.now()` only on the first receive of a given tool_call_id (line 757) and is then idempotent (line 741) — so on a long disconnect, the original startedAt is preserved across replay. While the disconnect was open, `nowMs` advanced (or the tab was backgrounded and the setInterval throttled, then thawed on focus), and `Math.max(nowMs - startedAt, liveElapsedMs)` returns the inflated value. liveElapsedMs is sourced from ToolProgress events that no production code emits (see finding feature-2-006), so the floor never updates. Heartbeat frames (web/streaming.py:148) carry only `ts` — no tool_call_id, not ring-buffered, not consumed by the reducer. Two coupled gaps: (a) ToolCallStart needs a server-reported started_at that the reducer adopts on REPLAY (replacing the client estimate), or (b) ToolProgress (finding feature-2-006) needs to be emitted with a server-derived elapsed_ms that floors the local computation. Either path closes the spec gap. STILL_OPEN.
suggested_files_to_change: src/bearings/agent/events.py (add `started_at: float` to ToolCallStart, or rely on ToolProgress per finding feature-2-006)
src/bearings/agent/runner.py / sdk_loop.py (set started_at at tool-call dispatch)
frontend/src/lib/stores/conversation.svelte.ts (replace client startedAt with server-derived value on replay)
acceptance_criteria: ToolCallStart events carry a server-set `started_at` (epoch seconds) OR ToolProgress events deliver a server-derived `elapsed_ms` (per finding feature-2-006) that the frontend treats as authoritative on replay.
On reconnect after a long disconnect, the elapsed-time readout for an in-flight tool reflects (now − server_started_at), not (now − first_client_receive_at).
Live-phase ticking remains client-driven (1s setInterval) so the cadence stays smooth between server ticks.
A vitest case simulates: ToolCallStart received, disconnect for 30s with timer suspended, reconnect with replay, asserts elapsedMs == ~30s rather than 0 or some inflated value.
executor_brief: Elapsed time inflates on long-disconnect replay because startedAt is fixed at the first client receive of ToolCallStart and never updated by a server-authoritative tick. Either add `started_at` to ToolCallStart (cheaper, single round-trip) or land ToolProgress emission per finding feature-2-006 and treat its elapsed_ms as the floor on replay. Update the conversation reducer + ToolOutput to honour the server value, add a vitest covering the disconnect-then-replay scenario.

audit_text: Tool-work drawer always starts open (<details open>). Spec implies user can collapse during streaming; that scenario is currently impossible.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/tool-output-streaming.md §"Scroll-anchor behavior" — "A previously-collapsed drawer. When the drawer was collapsed during streaming and the user later wants to inspect it, the assistant bubble carries a small '⤴ TOOLS' jump button that opens the drawer and scrolls it into view."; chat.md §"What a message turn looks like" — "Tool-work drawer (collapsible <details>)".
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/components/conversation/MessageTurn.svelte:425 (`<details ... open bind:this={toolWorkEl}>` — `open` is hard-coded, no preference store, no condition)
verdict_evidence: MessageTurn.svelte:425 hard-codes the `open` attribute on the tool-work `<details>` element. There is no reactive state, preference, or ref-binding governing the initial open/closed state — every assistant turn renders with the drawer pre-expanded, so the spec's 'collapsed during streaming' branch (which the '⤴ TOOLS' jump button is described to handle) is unreachable on first render. The user can collapse the drawer manually after the fact, but a fresh turn re-expands by default. STILL_OPEN.
suggested_files_to_change: frontend/src/lib/stores/preferences.svelte.ts (or wherever per-user UI prefs live) — optional preference for default-collapsed drawer
acceptance_criteria: The tool-work drawer's initial open state is driven by either a per-user preference (default open or default collapsed) or a per-turn signal (e.g. open while the turn is in flight, persisted state thereafter), NOT a hard-coded HTML attribute.
The user can collapse the drawer mid-stream and the collapsed state survives the rest of the streaming turn (re-renders do not auto-reopen).
When collapsed, the assistant bubble shows the '⤴ TOOLS' jump button described in the spec; clicking it expands the drawer and scrolls it into view.
A vitest case asserts the user-collapsed state persists across at least one tool_output_delta re-render.
executor_brief: MessageTurn.svelte:425 hard-codes `<details open>` on the tool-work drawer, which makes the spec's 'collapsed during streaming' code path (and the ⤴ TOOLS jump button that depends on it) unreachable on first render. Replace the static `open` attribute with reactive state (per-turn signal or per-user preference), wire the ⤴ TOOLS jump button to expand+scroll the collapsed drawer, and add a vitest covering the collapsed-mid-stream path.

audit_text: BearingsSessionStore adapter. sdk_loop.py references it in a comment for history replay; import/implementation unconfirmed. Suspected 'module exists, unwired' — needs grep verification.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/chat.md §"The agent loop start/stop semantics" — "History persistence across respawn. Every time the agent's underlying SDK subprocess writes a transcript line, Bearings mirrors it to durable storage. When the supervisor respawns the subprocess (model swap, idle reap, server restart, recovery from ERROR), the new subprocess inherits the full conversation context". docs/architecture-v1.md and CHANGELOG.md L19 reference the SessionStore adapter wiring.
code_paths_checked: src/bearings/agent/session_store.py:74-160 (BearingsSessionStore class definition + __all__)
src/bearings/agent/session_bootstrap.py:43 (`from bearings.agent.session_store import BearingsSessionStore`)
src/bearings/agent/session_bootstrap.py:159 (`store = BearingsSessionStore(db_factory=db_factory)`)
src/bearings/agent/session_bootstrap.py:170-186 (compose_session_options(..., session_store=store, sdk_session_id=..., resume=...))
src/bearings/agent/sdk_loop.py:453-464 (kwargs.session_store forwarded into ClaudeAgentOptions as `session_store`)
tests/test_session_bootstrap_resume.py:70,94,115,116 (asserts isinstance(options.session_store, BearingsSessionStore))
tests/test_session_store.py:54-60 (BearingsSessionStore unit tests)
CHANGELOG.md:19
verdict_evidence: BearingsSessionStore is defined at session_store.py:74, imported at session_bootstrap.py:43, instantiated at session_bootstrap.py:159, passed to compose_session_options as `session_store=store` (L183), and forwarded into the SDK kwargs at sdk_loop.py:459-460 (`if kwargs.session_store is not None: sdk_kwargs['session_store'] = kwargs.session_store`). Resume routing is in place: prior_entry_count > 0 → resume=<sdk_uuid>; else sdk_session_id=<sdk_uuid>. test_session_bootstrap_resume.py asserts all four of: bootstrap-attaches-store on first spawn, resumes on second spawn, fresh-and-resumed both carry the store. The CHANGELOG L19 entry references this wiring as 'lands the model-swap context-loss fix diagnosed 2026-05-05'. The audit's suspicion ('module exists, unwired') does not reproduce in HEAD. No characteristic line indicates a regression. Resolving commit not pinned (CHANGELOG cites the wiring landing pre-audit; the audit text itself flagged this as a verification-needed item rather than a confirmed bug).
